content.full_text:
GORDON C. PHILLIPS
CITY ATTORNEY

TELEPHONE
(310) 318-0655
FAX: (310) 372-3886

JUL 27 [illegible] 92

CITY OF REDONDO BEACH
CALIFORNIA
415 DIAMOND STREET
POST OFFICE BOX 270
REDONDO BEACH, CALIFORNIA 90277-0270

July 16, 1992


Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95807-0807

Attention: Blanca M. Breeze

Re: Your File # I-91-264

Dear Ms. Breeze:

     This letter is to follow up on our discussions of that
portion of your Advice Letter to Gloria Valenti in which you
opined that the word "Mayor" should be deleted from any portion
of the notice in which reference is made to a hearing "before the
Mayor and City Council".

     The legislative body of the City of Redondo Beach is
comprised of a 5-person City Council, each of whom is elected
from a district.

     The Mayor is elected from the City at large. The Mayor may
veto any action of the City Council, or vote in case of a tie.

     The legislative Counsel has advised the City that for
purposes of the Brown Act, the Mayor is to be considered in
determining whether or not a quorum is present for purposes of
the Act.

     The City has traditionally recognized the Mayor as a
participant in hearings. For example, it has been the practice,
in the adoption of Resolutions, to state: "The Mayor and City
Council Do Hereby Resolve...." I have enclosed a copy of a
recent resolution granting a land use entitlement which clearly
illustrates the notice and the grant of the entitlement are
congruent.

Fair Political Practices Commission
Attention: Blanca M. Breeze
July 16, 1992
Page 2




Since the Mayor is not part of the legislative body, and
because he/she may veto any action or vote to break a tie, he is,
indeed, a person before whom the hearing is held. It would be
inappropriate, in view of the charter, to include him/her as part
of the City Council. To prevent the legal notiices from affording
the Mayor is one of the participants in a hearing who may affect
its outcome exhaults form over substance.

I would respectfully request that you amend your advice
letter to delete that portion which prohibits reference to the
Mayor in the Notice of Hearing.

I have enclosed relevant portions of the Redondo Beach City
Charter for your review.

Thank you for your consideration of this matter.

Very truly yours,


Gordon C. Phillips
City Attorney

GCP:jme
enclosures

cc:  Mayor and City Council
     Gloria Valenti, City Clerk's Office

embedding.qa_text: July 16, 1992 Fair Political Practices Commission P.O. Box 807 Sacramento, California 95807-0807 Attention: Blanca M. Breeze Re: Your File # I-91-264 Dear Ms. Breeze: This letter is to follow up on our discussions of that portion of your Advice Letter to Gloria Valenti in which you opined that the word "Mayor" should be deleted from any portion of the notice in which reference is made to a hearing "before the Mayor and City Council". The legislative body of the City of Redondo Beach is comprised of a 5-person City Council, each of whom is elected from a district. The Mayor is elected from the City at large. The Mayor may veto any action of the City Council, or vote in case of a tie. The Legislative Counsel has advised the City that for purposes of the Brown Act, the Mayor is to be considered in determining whether or not a quorum is present for purposes of the Act. The City has traditionally recognized the Mayor as a participant in hearings. For example, it has been the practice, in the adoption of Resolutions, to state: "The Mayor and City Council Do Hereby Resolve....". I have enclosed a copy of a recent resolution granting a land use entitlement which clearly illustrates the notice and the grant of the entitlement are congruent. Fair Political Practices Commission Attention: Blanca M. Breeze July 16, 1992 Page 2 Since the Mayor is not part of the legislative body, and because he/she may veto any action or vote to break a tie, he is, indeed, a person before whom the hearing is held. It would be inappropriate, in view of the charter, to include him/her as part of the City Council. To prevent the legal notices from indicating the Mayor is one of the participants in a hearing who may affect its outcome exhausts form over substance. I would respectfully request that you amend your advice letter to delete that portion which prohibits reference to the Mayor in the Notice of Hearing. I have enclosed relevant portions of the Redondo Beach City Charter for your review. Thank you for your consideration of this matter. Very truly yours, Gordon C. Phillips City Attorney GCP:jme enclosures cc: Mayor and City Council Gloria Valenti, City Clerk's Office ca>ltr$>gordon*>breeze.I-91-264.
